back_translation: Now Ahab had seventy sons in Samaria. So Jehu wrote letters and sent them to Samaria, to the officials of Jezreel, to the elders, and to the guardians of Ahab’s sons, saying:
“When this letter comes to you, since your master’s sons are with you and you have chariots, horses, a fortified city, and weapons,
choose the best and most fitting of your master’s sons, set him on his father’s throne, and fight for your master’s house.”
But they were terrified and said, “Since two kings could not stand against him, how can we?”

So the palace administrator, the governor of the city, the elders, and the guardians sent to Jehu, saying, “We are your servants; we will do all you say. We will make no one king; do what is good in your sight.”

Then Jehu wrote them a second letter, saying, “If you are on my side and will obey me, bring me the heads of your master’s sons at Jezreel by this time tomorrow.” Now the seventy sons of the king were with the leading men of the city, who were raising them.
When the letter came, they took the king’s sons and slaughtered all seventy, put their heads in baskets, and sent them to Jehu at Jezreel.
When the messenger came and told Jehu, “They have brought the heads of the king’s sons,” he said, “Pile them in two heaps at the entrance of the gate until morning.”
In the morning Jehu went out and stood before all the people and said, “You are innocent. It was I who conspired against my master and killed him; but who killed all these?
Know then that not one word the LORD has spoken against the house of Ahab will fail, for the LORD has done what He spoke through His servant Elijah.”
So Jehu killed all who remained of the house of Ahab in Jezreel, along with all his great men, close friends, and priests, until he left him no survivor.
Then Jehu set out and went toward Samaria. On the way, at Beth-eked of the Shepherds,
Jehu met the relatives of Ahaziah king of Judah and asked, “Who are you?” They answered, “We are relatives of Ahaziah, and we have come down to greet the sons of the king and of the queen mother.”
Jehu said, “Take them alive!” So they took them alive and slaughtered them at the well of Beth-eked—forty-two men. He left none of them.

When he left there, he found Jehonadab son of Rechab coming to meet him. Jehu greeted him and asked, “Is your heart true to mine as my heart is to yours?” Jehonadab answered, “It is.” “If so,” said Jehu, “give me your hand.” So he gave him his hand, and Jehu pulled him up into the chariot,
saying, “Come with me and see my zeal for the LORD.” So he had him ride in his chariot.
When Jehu came to Samaria, he killed all who remained of Ahab there, until he had destroyed them, according to the word the LORD had spoken to Elijah.
Then Jehu gathered all the people and said, “Ahab served Baal a little, but Jehu will serve him much.

Now therefore summon to me all the prophets of Baal, all his servants, and all his priests; let no one be missing, for I have a great sacrifice for Baal. Whoever is missing will not live.” But Jehu was acting deceptively in order to destroy the servants of Baal.
Jehu said, “Proclaim a solemn assembly for Baal.” So they proclaimed it.
Jehu sent throughout Israel, and all the servants of Baal came; there was no one who did not come. They entered the temple of Baal, and it was filled from end to end.
Jehu said to the keeper of the wardrobe, “Bring out garments for all the servants of Baal.” So he brought out garments for them.

Then Jehu and Jehonadab son of Rechab went into the temple of Baal, and Jehu said to the servants of Baal, “Search and see that there are no servants of the LORD here among you, only servants of Baal.”
They went in to offer sacrifices and burnt offerings. Now Jehu had stationed eighty men outside and warned them, “If any of the men I deliver into your hands escapes, he will pay with his own life.”

When he had finished offering the burnt offering, Jehu said to the guards and officers, “Go in and kill them; let no one escape.” So they put them to the sword, threw out the bodies, and went into the inner room of the temple of Baal.
They brought out the sacred pillar of the temple of Baal and burned it.
They demolished the sacred pillar of Baal, and they tore down the temple of Baal and made it a latrine, which it is to this day.
Thus Jehu eradicated Baal from Israel.
But Jehu did not turn from the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, who caused Israel to sin—the worship of the golden calves at Bethel and at Dan.

Nevertheless the LORD said to Jehu, “Because you have done well in doing what is right in My eyes, and have done to the house of Ahab all that was in My heart, your sons to the fourth generation will sit on the throne of Israel.”
But Jehu was not careful to walk in the law of the LORD, the God of Israel, with all his heart; he did not turn from the sins of Jeroboam, who caused Israel to sin.
In those days the LORD began to cut Israel short, and Hazael struck them throughout the territory of Israel,
from the Jordan eastward, all the land of Gilead (the region of Gad, Reuben, and Manasseh), from Aroer by the Arnon Valley, through Gilead to Bashan.
As for the rest of the acts of Jehu, all that he did, and all his might, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel?
And Jehu rested with his fathers and was buried in Samaria, and his son Jehoahaz reigned in his place.
The time that Jehu reigned over Israel in Samaria was twenty-eight years.
